method.h inclusion macro

#define METHOD_ARGUMENTS_BITS   (4)

Defines the number of bits of a method index used for encoding the number of arguments the method accepts

Definition at line 45 of file method.h.

Referenced by method_create_packed_index().

#define METHOD_ARGUMENTS_MASK   (METHOD_ARGUMENTS_MAX - 1)

Defines a bit mask to extract the number of arguments of a method

Definition at line 51 of file method.h.

#define METHOD_ARGUMENTS_MAX   (1 << METHOD_ARGUMENTS_BITS)

Defines the maximum number of arguments accepted by a method

Definition at line 48 of file method.h.

Referenced by parse_method_descriptor().

#define METHOD_INDEX_BITS   (12)

Defines the number of bits of a method index used for encoding the actual index in the dispatch table

Definition at line 55 of file method.h.

#define METHOD_INDEX_MAX   (1 << METHOD_INDEX_BITS)

Defines the maximum number of methods which can be declared by a class

Definition at line 58 of file method.h.

#define METHOD_INDEX_SHIFT   (METHOD_ARGUMENTS_BITS)

Defines the shift value used to extract the actual method index from the index encoded in the bytecode

Definition at line 62 of file method.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum return_value_t

Represents the basic return type of a method, currently used only for native methods

Enumerator:
RET_VOID 

No return value.

RET_INT 

boolean, byte, char, short or int return value

RET_LONG 

long return value

RET_OBJECT 

Object reference return value.

RET_FLOAT 

Float return value.

RET_DOUBLE 

Double return value.

Definition at line 67 of file method.h.

Variable Documentation

method_t halt_method

Dummy method used to halt the interpreter

Basically the main() method of the first thread (or the run() method of user-created threads) return to this method when they are done and this method halts the interpreter execution. This method also contains a fake exception handler which catches all possible exceptions and then deals with them as uncatched exceptions

Halt method structure

Definition at line 54 of file method.c.

Referenced by interpreter(), and prepare_for_call().
